title: rosa beggeriana var. typica
intro: Rosa beggeriana var. typica, also known as the Begger's Rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to China and considered an endangered species due to over-collection for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and flowers are used in traditional Chinese medicine, where they are valued for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa beggeriana var. typica include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as being used to treat respiratory and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and flowers are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a powder to extract its medicinal compounds.
